schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: FEMA Compliance — FDI, ECB, ODI | Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: FEMA compliance by CA firm. FDI reporting (FC-GPR, FC-TRS), ECB compliance, ODI advisory, LRS guidance, compounding applications. Cross-border regulatory navigator since 2012.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late FC-GPR filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late FC-GPR filing constitutes a FEMA contravention under Section 15 of FEMA, 1999. Compounding fees can be up to 3 times the amount involved. We handle compounding applications with documented mitigation strategies, consistently achieving penalties below the statutory maximum.

Yes. Full ECB lifecycle: structuring advice, all-in-cost ceiling compliance, ECB-2 monthly filing, end-use monitoring, conversion of ECB to equity, and RBI approval applications for amounts exceeding automatic route limits.

mainEntity.name: Can the same valuation work for both FEMA and Income Tax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not always. FEMA mandates DCF for unlisted companies. Rule 11UA accepts the higher of NAV and DCF. When NAV exceeds DCF, the values diverge. We prepare dual-framework reports that satisfy both regulators from a single engagement.

Overseas Direct Investment compliance covers Indian companies investing abroad — JVs, WOS, step-down subsidiaries. Includes ODI reporting via FIRMS portal, Annual Performance Report, disinvestment compliance, and financial commitment computation under the 2022 Rules.

mainEntity.name: How long does FEMA compounding take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Compounding applications filed with the RBI regional office typically take 3-6 months to process. We prepare the application, compute likely compounding fees, present mitigating factors, and represent before the Compounding Authority.

mainEntity.name: Is FEMA compliance needed for a company with NRI shareholders?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends. NRI investment on repatriation basis is treated as FDI and requires FEMA compliance. NRI investment on non-repatriation basis follows a different (simplified) route. The distinction determines filing obligations. We assess the cap table and advise on the correct compliance path.
